prefix_anonymized: 2014: not sure I would call them museums more like obstacle courses when they are slick after rain! visually but can be pretty impractical
character's fine but thinking about people with mobility issues makes one wish there was less climbing involved
The area totally needs parks too!

pretty visuals do not always mean good function – some places look amazing at first glance but do not get me started on how they blend into surroundings...or do not always feels like design overtook function without asking anyone if it actually works
not sure those fleeting chill vibes really offset daily rush hour madness, feels like putting a cute bandaid on a stress fracture sometimes...still busy over here

kinda feel like 'recharging' might need more than just those tiny pauses when your whole day's steeped in hectic moments though sometimes it takes real downtime where you're not just playing catch-up with your own breath
not that simple always - even balancing acts get tough in non-stop life
not everyone gets amped up by constant activity tbh... some thrive off quiet moments tucked away in those rare still spots around. gotta balance it out instead of just getting swept up yknow?
definitely more fun than factual in my opinion
old buildings got their fair share of ghost rumors too lol
love those random high-up views where everything else feels distant

totally get you on those cafe vibes - there's a small bridge here that crosses nothing more than a trickle most times but somehow its modern lines against old cobblestones just works magic for me every morning commute
adds character though, don't you think?
totally see both sides here - those quaint spots are worth some extra love though. right balance makes them priceless nooks over time & they kinda become part of a daily zen ritual!
love how rain makes surfaces look like art installations

gotta say i'm kinda on a different page - growing hasn't made me swap exploring for aprons just yet lol! always thought our passions don't "shift," maybe they expand? like adding layers rather than changing gears completely...
routine can be comforting but also cages creativity imo

interesting take but nah for me it's been the opposite - thoughts on architecture have got way bigger since earlier days; who even has time for cozy settings when design kicks up? living spaces are projects not just chill zones right?

sure historical context matters bt honestly keeping ahead with innovative designs & pushing boundaries? way cooler imo especially seeing everyday spaces turn into something groundbreaking 😎 constant evolution over nostalgia any day!
stable? feels like standing still when you could be soaring skies with new designs!

everyone's acing adult stuff here lol... i'm just impressed when i don't kill my plants 😂 seems low bar but hey green thumbs aren't distributed at birth right? gotta celebrate not turning them crispy!
meh, try beating procrastination on renewing IDs – epic hassle saver!

making appointments never felt like an achievement for me – just part of daily routine you gotta handle. isn't that just basic self-management? interesting though how we all measure our wins differently!

not sure those "ideal" milestones hold up anymore tbh, feels like they're relics of a different era - everyone's journey's unique. working decent jobs doesn't mean rushing into property ladders n all that jazz imo 🏠💸
norms are overrated but hard to ignore

honestly think society's timeline is outta touch...like owning property at a certain age? crazy talk in today's market unless you're raking in big bucks 🤷 just gotta roll at your own pace 'n let things fall into place naturally
goalposts turning into mirages
mortgage application was like leveling up in adulting – felt i'd hit boss mode handling all that paperwork 😬
guess people forget money isn't everything
honestly think cash just highlights who was fake all along - those are real tests of friendship even over here.artist? architect? doesn't matter - true colors will show eventually when success hits.
not sure everyone's acting all weird just cause you're doing well - sometimes folks are just dealing with their own stuff y'know? can't assume everyone's motives are linked to your success tbh.
not all mates turn weird tho.
even careful spending feels stretched thin these days
cutting back too, still hopeful though!
totally see your point but i think we gotta play smarter inside this tight economy game somehow

felt pretty set on a path after studies & yeah still working in that area today but definitely learned it ain't just drawing all day - lots of teamwork & unexpected challenges! always evolving even if you stick with your focus 👍
studied design stuff - now sketching plans & loving it!

i stuck pretty close to my degree actually! always had a thing for designs & creating spaces where stories unfold 🏡 sure sometimes i think 'bout changing lanes but guess i'm happiest shaping city skylines & cozy nooks ☕ might not sound as wild as swapping marine bio for app development but hey stability has its own kind of charm ya know? 🌆
seems we both found our niche early on - nice!
arch grad here - defs thought design work would be more planned out but it's all improv these days lol always morphin' projects & meetin' weird client wishes keeps me on point though
school seemed straight-line… real world? ha! not quite

haha yep totally get it, who knew all those hours sketchin' would lead me down such wild project rabbit holes instead just fancy buildings - jobs list never mentioned "psychic" 🙈! gotta roll with it though right?
literature sounds super versatile 👏 i'm finding creativity's key no matter the degree!

totally feels familiar! had to sketch out floor plans by hand once cause our software crashed during revision delivery… clients actually loved the 'personal touch' turned potential disaster into win lol
used coffee stirrers once as temporary model support during a surprise client visit - talk about winging it!

had something similar happen during reviews when our 3d printer bailed on us - ended up salvaging the situation using paperclips & spare fabric samples! these fixes can really save your skin sometimes 😂

totally feel you on seeing projects come alive! finished designing this community center last year & watching people enjoy the space i envisioned... man it's unreal! always gets me when functionality meets aesthetics just right 👌🏼
designing places gives you real insight into how much surroundings affect moods & choices

sculpting spaces really tunes you into details - like every line can spark joy or soothe souls. found myself appreciating small design choices around me more since i started drafting plans; it's kind wild ¯\_(ツ)_/¯

arch grad here - defs thought design work would be more planned out but it's all improv these days lol always morphin' projects & meetin' weird client wishes keeps me on point though
school seemed straight-line… real world? ha! not quite

haha yep totally get it, who knew all those hours sketchin' would lead me down such wild project rabbit holes instead just fancy buildings - jobs list never mentioned "psychic" 🙈! gotta roll with it though right?
literature sounds super versatile 👏 i'm finding creativity's key no matter the degree!

totally feels familiar! had to sketch out floor plans by hand once cause our software crashed during revision delivery… clients actually loved the 'personal touch' turned potential disaster into win lol
used coffee stirrers once as temporary model support during a surprise client visit - talk about winging it!

had something similar happen during reviews when our 3d printer bailed on us - ended up salvaging the situation using paperclips & spare fabric samples! these fixes can really save your skin sometimes 😂

totally feel you on seeing projects come alive! finished designing this community center last year & watching people enjoy the space i envisioned... man it's unreal! always gets me when functionality meets aesthetics just right 👌🏼
designing places gives you real insight into how much surroundings affect moods & choices

sculpting spaces really tunes you into details - like every line can spark joy or soothe souls. found myself appreciating small design choices around me more since i started drafting plans; it's kind wild ¯\_(ツ)_/¯
design shapes society but people matter more tbh

rounds.anonymized: design shapes society but people matter more tbh
prefix_anonymized: 2014: not sure I would call them museums more like obstacle courses when they are slick after rain! visually but can be pretty impractical
character's fine but thinking about people with mobility issues makes one wish there was less climbing involved
The area totally needs parks too!

pretty visuals do not always mean good function – some places look amazing at first glance but do not get me started on how they blend into surroundings...or do not always feels like design overtook function without asking anyone if it actually works
not sure those fleeting chill vibes really offset daily rush hour madness, feels like putting a cute bandaid on a stress fracture sometimes...still busy over here

kinda feel like 'recharging' might need more than just those tiny pauses when your whole day's steeped in hectic moments though sometimes it takes real downtime where you're not just playing catch-up with your own breath
not that simple always - even balancing acts get tough in non-stop life
not everyone gets amped up by constant activity tbh... some thrive off quiet moments tucked away in those rare still spots around. gotta balance it out instead of just getting swept up yknow?
definitely more fun than factual in my opinion
old buildings got their fair share of ghost rumors too lol
love those random high-up views where everything else feels distant

totally get you on those cafe vibes - there's a small bridge here that crosses nothing more than a trickle most times but somehow its modern lines against old cobblestones just works magic for me every morning commute
adds character though, don't you think?
totally see both sides here - those quaint spots are worth some extra love though. right balance makes them priceless nooks over time & they kinda become part of a daily zen ritual!
love how rain makes surfaces look like art installations

gotta say i'm kinda on a different page - growing hasn't made me swap exploring for aprons just yet lol! always thought our passions don't "shift," maybe they expand? like adding layers rather than changing gears completely...
routine can be comforting but also cages creativity imo

interesting take but nah for me it's been the opposite - thoughts on architecture have got way bigger since earlier days; who even has time for cozy settings when design kicks up? living spaces are projects not just chill zones right?

sure historical context matters bt honestly keeping ahead with innovative designs & pushing boundaries? way cooler imo especially seeing everyday spaces turn into something groundbreaking 😎 constant evolution over nostalgia any day!
stable? feels like standing still when you could be soaring skies with new designs!

everyone's acing adult stuff here lol... i'm just impressed when i don't kill my plants 😂 seems low bar but hey green thumbs aren't distributed at birth right? gotta celebrate not turning them crispy!
meh, try beating procrastination on renewing IDs – epic hassle saver!

making appointments never felt like an achievement for me – just part of daily routine you gotta handle. isn't that just basic self-management? interesting though how we all measure our wins differently!

not sure those "ideal" milestones hold up anymore tbh, feels like they're relics of a different era - everyone's journey's unique. working decent jobs doesn't mean rushing into property ladders n all that jazz imo 🏠💸
norms are overrated but hard to ignore

honestly think society's timeline is outta touch...like owning property at a certain age? crazy talk in today's market unless you're raking in big bucks 🤷 just gotta roll at your own pace 'n let things fall into place naturally
goalposts turning into mirages
mortgage application was like leveling up in adulting – felt i'd hit boss mode handling all that paperwork 😬
guess people forget money isn't everything
honestly think cash just highlights who was fake all along - those are real tests of friendship even over here.artist? architect? doesn't matter - true colors will show eventually when success hits.
not sure everyone's acting all weird just cause you're doing well - sometimes folks are just dealing with their own stuff y'know? can't assume everyone's motives are linked to your success tbh.
not all mates turn weird tho.
even careful spending feels stretched thin these days
cutting back too, still hopeful though!
totally see your point but i think we gotta play smarter inside this tight economy game somehow

comments.original: crazy right? studied dreaming of iconic skyscrapers - now i'm more into sustainability techs changing our skyline subtly! unwind doodling tiny cabins though... anyone else find solace back in small scale after a day juggling codes & clients?

rounds.anonymized: crazy right? studied dreaming of iconic skyscrapers - now i'm more into sustainability techs changing our skyline subtly! unwind doodling tiny cabins though... anyone else find solace back in small scale after a day juggling codes & clients?
prefix_anonymized: 2014: not sure I would call them museums more like obstacle courses when they are slick after rain! visually but can be pretty impractical
character's fine but thinking about people with mobility issues makes one wish there was less climbing involved
The area totally needs parks too!

pretty visuals do not always mean good function – some places look amazing at first glance but do not get me started on how they blend into surroundings...or do not always feels like design overtook function without asking anyone if it actually works
not sure those fleeting chill vibes really offset daily rush hour madness, feels like putting a cute bandaid on a stress fracture sometimes...still busy over here

kinda feel like 'recharging' might need more than just those tiny pauses when your whole day's steeped in hectic moments though sometimes it takes real downtime where you're not just playing catch-up with your own breath
not that simple always - even balancing acts get tough in non-stop life
not everyone gets amped up by constant activity tbh... some thrive off quiet moments tucked away in those rare still spots around. gotta balance it out instead of just getting swept up yknow?
definitely more fun than factual in my opinion
old buildings got their fair share of ghost rumors too lol
love those random high-up views where everything else feels distant

totally get you on those cafe vibes - there's a small bridge here that crosses nothing more than a trickle most times but somehow its modern lines against old cobblestones just works magic for me every morning commute
adds character though, don't you think?
totally see both sides here - those quaint spots are worth some extra love though. right balance makes them priceless nooks over time & they kinda become part of a daily zen ritual!
love how rain makes surfaces look like art installations

gotta say i'm kinda on a different page - growing hasn't made me swap exploring for aprons just yet lol! always thought our passions don't "shift," maybe they expand? like adding layers rather than changing gears completely...
routine can be comforting but also cages creativity imo

interesting take but nah for me it's been the opposite - thoughts on architecture have got way bigger since earlier days; who even has time for cozy settings when design kicks up? living spaces are projects not just chill zones right?

sure historical context matters bt honestly keeping ahead with innovative designs & pushing boundaries? way cooler imo especially seeing everyday spaces turn into something groundbreaking 😎 constant evolution over nostalgia any day!
stable? feels like standing still when you could be soaring skies with new designs!

everyone's acing adult stuff here lol... i'm just impressed when i don't kill my plants 😂 seems low bar but hey green thumbs aren't distributed at birth right? gotta celebrate not turning them crispy!
meh, try beating procrastination on renewing IDs – epic hassle saver!

making appointments never felt like an achievement for me – just part of daily routine you gotta handle. isn't that just basic self-management? interesting though how we all measure our wins differently!

not sure those "ideal" milestones hold up anymore tbh, feels like they're relics of a different era - everyone's journey's unique. working decent jobs doesn't mean rushing into property ladders n all that jazz imo 🏠💸
norms are overrated but hard to ignore

honestly think society's timeline is outta touch...like owning property at a certain age? crazy talk in today's market unless you're raking in big bucks 🤷 just gotta roll at your own pace 'n let things fall into place naturally
goalposts turning into mirages
mortgage application was like leveling up in adulting – felt i'd hit boss mode handling all that paperwork 😬
guess people forget money isn't everything
honestly think cash just highlights who was fake all along - those are real tests of friendship even over here.artist? architect? doesn't matter - true colors will show eventually when success hits.
not sure everyone's acting all weird just cause you're doing well - sometimes folks are just dealing with their own stuff y'know? can't assume everyone's motives are linked to your success tbh.
not all mates turn weird tho.
even careful spending feels stretched thin these days
cutting back too, still hopeful though!
totally see your point but i think we gotta play smarter inside this tight economy game somehow

felt pretty set on a path after studies & yeah still working in that area today but definitely learned it ain't just drawing all day - lots of teamwork & unexpected challenges! always evolving even if you stick with your focus 👍
studied design stuff - now sketching plans & loving it!

i stuck pretty close to my degree actually! always had a thing for designs & creating spaces where stories unfold 🏡 sure sometimes i think 'bout changing lanes but guess i'm happiest shaping city skylines & cozy nooks ☕ might not sound as wild as swapping marine bio for app development but hey stability has its own kind of charm ya know? 🌆
seems we both found our niche early on - nice!
arch grad here - defs thought design work would be more planned out but it's all improv these days lol always morphin' projects & meetin' weird client wishes keeps me on point though
school seemed straight-line… real world? ha! not quite

haha yep totally get it, who knew all those hours sketchin' would lead me down such wild project rabbit holes instead just fancy buildings - jobs list never mentioned "psychic" 🙈! gotta roll with it though right?
literature sounds super versatile 👏 i'm finding creativity's key no matter the degree!

totally feels familiar! had to sketch out floor plans by hand once cause our software crashed during revision delivery… clients actually loved the 'personal touch' turned potential disaster into win lol
used coffee stirrers once as temporary model support during a surprise client visit - talk about winging it!

had something similar happen during reviews when our 3d printer bailed on us - ended up salvaging the situation using paperclips & spare fabric samples! these fixes can really save your skin sometimes 😂

totally feel you on seeing projects come alive! finished designing this community center last year & watching people enjoy the space i envisioned... man it's unreal! always gets me when functionality meets aesthetics just right 👌🏼
designing places gives you real insight into how much surroundings affect moods & choices

sculpting spaces really tunes you into details - like every line can spark joy or soothe souls. found myself appreciating small design choices around me more since i started drafting plans; it's kind wild ¯\_(ツ)_/¯
design shapes society but people matter more tbh

architecture seemed all creative until it turned into playing tetris with spaces and client demands - really switches up your weekend plans when you're dreaming of optimizations instead of chill netflix binge sessions!

crazy right? studied dreaming of iconic skyscrapers - now i'm more into sustainability techs changing our skyline subtly! unwind doodling tiny cabins though... anyone else find solace back in small scale after a day juggling codes & clients?

goalposts turning into mirages

honestly think cash just highlights who was fake all along - those are real tests of friendship even over here.artist? architect? doesn't matter - true colors will show eventually when success hits.

even careful spending feels stretched thin these days
cutting back too, still hopeful though!

felt pretty set on a path after studies & yeah still working in that area today but definitely learned it ain't just drawing all day - lots of teamwork & unexpected challenges! always evolving even if you stick with your focus 👍
studied design stuff - now sketching plans & loving it!

i stuck pretty close to my degree actually! always had a thing for designs & creating spaces where stories unfold 🏡 sure sometimes i think 'bout changing lanes but guess i'm happiest shaping city skylines & cozy nooks ☕ might not sound as wild as swapping marine bio for app development but hey stability has its own kind of charm ya know? 🌆
seems we both found our niche early on - nice!
arch grad here - defs thought design work would be more planned out but it's all improv these days lol always morphin' projects & meetin' weird client wishes keeps me on point though
school seemed straight-line… real world? ha! not quite

haha yep totally get it, who knew all those hours sketchin' would lead me down such wild project rabbit holes instead just fancy buildings - jobs list never mentioned "psychic" 🙈! gotta roll with it though right?
literature sounds super versatile 👏 i'm finding creativity's key no matter the degree!

totally feels familiar! had to sketch out floor plans by hand once cause our software crashed during revision delivery… clients actually loved the 'personal touch' turned potential disaster into win lol
used coffee stirrers once as temporary model support during a surprise client visit - talk about winging it!

had something similar happen during reviews when our 3d printer bailed on us - ended up salvaging the situation using paperclips & spare fabric samples! these fixes can really save your skin sometimes 😂

totally feel you on seeing projects come alive! finished designing this community center last year & watching people enjoy the space i envisioned... man it's unreal! always gets me when functionality meets aesthetics just right 👌🏼
designing places gives you real insight into how much surroundings affect moods & choices

sculpting spaces really tunes you into details - like every line can spark joy or soothe souls. found myself appreciating small design choices around me more since i started drafting plans; it's kind wild ¯\_(ツ)_/¯
design shapes society but people matter more tbh

architecture seemed all creative until it turned into playing tetris with spaces and client demands - really switches up your weekend plans when you're dreaming of optimizations instead of chill netflix binge sessions!

crazy right? studied dreaming of iconic skyscrapers - now i'm more into sustainability techs changing our skyline subtly! unwind doodling tiny cabins though... anyone else find solace back in small scale after a day juggling codes & clients?
thought design was all creativity, paperwork proved me wrong lol

comments.original: so true! whoever said you can leave your job at the office never designed anything...i’ll catch myself judging buildings on walks like 'that facade needs a do-over'.
comments.anonymized: so true! whoever said you can leave your job at the office never designed anything...i'll catch myself judging buildings on walks like 'that facade needs a do-over'.

rounds.anonymized: so true! whoever said you can leave your job at the office never designed anything...i'll catch myself judging buildings on walks like 'that facade needs a do-over'.
prefix_anonymized: 2014: not sure I would call them museums more like obstacle courses when they are slick after rain! visually but can be pretty impractical
character's fine but thinking about people with mobility issues makes one wish there was less climbing involved
The area totally needs parks too!

pretty visuals do not always mean good function – some places look amazing at first glance but do not get me started on how they blend into surroundings...or do not always feels like design overtook function without asking anyone if it actually works
not sure those fleeting chill vibes really offset daily rush hour madness, feels like putting a cute bandaid on a stress fracture sometimes...still busy over here

kinda feel like 'recharging' might need more than just those tiny pauses when your whole day's steeped in hectic moments though sometimes it takes real downtime where you're not just playing catch-up with your own breath
not that simple always - even balancing acts get tough in non-stop life
not everyone gets amped up by constant activity tbh... some thrive off quiet moments tucked away in those rare still spots around. gotta balance it out instead of just getting swept up yknow?
definitely more fun than factual in my opinion
old buildings got their fair share of ghost rumors too lol
love those random high-up views where everything else feels distant

totally get you on those cafe vibes - there's a small bridge here that crosses nothing more than a trickle most times but somehow its modern lines against old cobblestones just works magic for me every morning commute
adds character though, don't you think?
totally see both sides here - those quaint spots are worth some extra love though. right balance makes them priceless nooks over time & they kinda become part of a daily zen ritual!
love how rain makes surfaces look like art installations

gotta say i'm kinda on a different page - growing hasn't made me swap exploring for aprons just yet lol! always thought our passions don't "shift," maybe they expand? like adding layers rather than changing gears completely...
routine can be comforting but also cages creativity imo

interesting take but nah for me it's been the opposite - thoughts on architecture have got way bigger since earlier days; who even has time for cozy settings when design kicks up? living spaces are projects not just chill zones right?

sure historical context matters bt honestly keeping ahead with innovative designs & pushing boundaries? way cooler imo especially seeing everyday spaces turn into something groundbreaking 😎 constant evolution over nostalgia any day!
stable? feels like standing still when you could be soaring skies with new designs!

everyone's acing adult stuff here lol... i'm just impressed when i don't kill my plants 😂 seems low bar but hey green thumbs aren't distributed at birth right? gotta celebrate not turning them crispy!
meh, try beating procrastination on renewing IDs – epic hassle saver!

making appointments never felt like an achievement for me – just part of daily routine you gotta handle. isn't that just basic self-management? interesting though how we all measure our wins differently!

not sure those "ideal" milestones hold up anymore tbh, feels like they're relics of a different era - everyone's journey's unique. working decent jobs doesn't mean rushing into property ladders n all that jazz imo 🏠💸
norms are overrated but hard to ignore

honestly think society's timeline is outta touch...like owning property at a certain age? crazy talk in today's market unless you're raking in big bucks 🤷 just gotta roll at your own pace 'n let things fall into place naturally
goalposts turning into mirages
mortgage application was like leveling up in adulting – felt i'd hit boss mode handling all that paperwork 😬
guess people forget money isn't everything
honestly think cash just highlights who was fake all along - those are real tests of friendship even over here.artist? architect? doesn't matter - true colors will show eventually when success hits.
not sure everyone's acting all weird just cause you're doing well - sometimes folks are just dealing with their own stuff y'know? can't assume everyone's motives are linked to your success tbh.
not all mates turn weird tho.
even careful spending feels stretched thin these days
cutting back too, still hopeful though!
totally see your point but i think we gotta play smarter inside this tight economy game somehow

felt pretty set on a path after studies & yeah still working in that area today but definitely learned it ain't just drawing all day - lots of teamwork & unexpected challenges! always evolving even if you stick with your focus 👍
studied design stuff - now sketching plans & loving it!

i stuck pretty close to my degree actually! always had a thing for designs & creating spaces where stories unfold 🏡 sure sometimes i think 'bout changing lanes but guess i'm happiest shaping city skylines & cozy nooks ☕ might not sound as wild as swapping marine bio for app development but hey stability has its own kind of charm ya know? 🌆
seems we both found our niche early on - nice!
arch grad here - defs thought design work would be more planned out but it's all improv these days lol always morphin' projects & meetin' weird client wishes keeps me on point though
school seemed straight-line… real world? ha! not quite

haha yep totally get it, who knew all those hours sketchin' would lead me down such wild project rabbit holes instead just fancy buildings - jobs list never mentioned "psychic" 🙈! gotta roll with it though right?
literature sounds super versatile 👏 i'm finding creativity's key no matter the degree!

totally feels familiar! had to sketch out floor plans by hand once cause our software crashed during revision delivery… clients actually loved the 'personal touch' turned potential disaster into win lol
used coffee stirrers once as temporary model support during a surprise client visit - talk about winging it!

had something similar happen during reviews when our 3d printer bailed on us - ended up salvaging the situation using paperclips & spare fabric samples! these fixes can really save your skin sometimes 😂

totally feel you on seeing projects come alive! finished designing this community center last year & watching people enjoy the space i envisioned... man it's unreal! always gets me when functionality meets aesthetics just right 👌🏼
designing places gives you real insight into how much surroundings affect moods & choices

sculpting spaces really tunes you into details - like every line can spark joy or soothe souls. found myself appreciating small design choices around me more since i started drafting plans; it's kind wild ¯\_(ツ)_/¯
design shapes society but people matter more tbh

architecture seemed all creative until it turned into playing tetris with spaces and client demands - really switches up your weekend plans when you're dreaming of optimizations instead of chill netflix binge sessions!

crazy right? studied dreaming of iconic skyscrapers - now i'm more into sustainability techs changing our skyline subtly! unwind doodling tiny cabins though... anyone else find solace back in small scale after a day juggling codes & clients?
thought design was all creativity, paperwork proved me wrong lol
meta but we're all trying aren't we?
so true! whoever said you can leave your job at the office never designed anything...i'll catch myself judging buildings on walks like 'that facade needs a do-over'.

goalposts turning into mirages

honestly think cash just highlights who was fake all along - those are real tests of friendship even over here.artist? architect? doesn't matter - true colors will show eventually when success hits.

cutting back too, still hopeful though!
totally see your point but i think we gotta play smarter inside this tight economy game somehow

felt pretty set on a path after studies & yeah still working in that area today but definitely learned it ain't just drawing all day - lots of teamwork & unexpected challenges! always evolving even if you stick with your focus 👍
studied design stuff - now sketching plans & loving it!

i stuck pretty close to my degree actually! always had a thing for designs & creating spaces where stories unfold 🏡 sure sometimes i think 'bout changing lanes but guess i'm happiest shaping city skylines & cozy nooks ☕ might not sound as wild as swapping marine bio for app development but hey stability has its own kind of charm ya know? 🌆
seems we both found our niche early on - nice!
arch grad here - defs thought design work would be more planned out but it's all improv these days lol always morphin' projects & meetin' weird client wishes keeps me on point though
school seemed straight-line… real world? ha! not quite

haha yep totally get it, who knew all those hours sketchin' would lead me down such wild project rabbit holes instead just fancy buildings - jobs list never mentioned "psychic" 🙈! gotta roll with it though right?
literature sounds super versatile 👏 i'm finding creativity's key no matter the degree!

totally feels familiar! had to sketch out floor plans by hand once cause our software crashed during revision delivery… clients actually loved the 'personal touch' turned potential disaster into win lol
used coffee stirrers once as temporary model support during a surprise client visit - talk about winging it!

had something similar happen during reviews when our 3d printer bailed on us - ended up salvaging the situation using paperclips & spare fabric samples! these fixes can really save your skin sometimes 😂

totally feel you on seeing projects come alive! finished designing this community center last year & watching people enjoy the space i envisioned... man it's unreal! always gets me when functionality meets aesthetics just right 👌🏼
designing places gives you real insight into how much surroundings affect moods & choices

sculpting spaces really tunes you into details - like every line can spark joy or soothe souls. found myself appreciating small design choices around me more since i started drafting plans; it's kind wild ¯\_(ツ)_/¯
design shapes society but people matter more tbh

architecture seemed all creative until it turned into playing tetris with spaces and client demands - really switches up your weekend plans when you're dreaming of optimizations instead of chill netflix binge sessions!

crazy right? studied dreaming of iconic skyscrapers - now i'm more into sustainability techs changing our skyline subtly! unwind doodling tiny cabins though... anyone else find solace back in small scale after a day juggling codes & clients?
thought design was all creativity, paperwork proved me wrong lol
meta but we're all trying aren't we?
so true! whoever said you can leave your job at the office never designed anything...i'll catch myself judging buildings on walks like 'that facade needs a do-over'.
it's getting better for sure! been part of some pretty cool projects where it was super collaborative - though gotta admit sometimes you still bump into those outdated views at site meetings 🙄
it's definitely getting better but still moments where you feel like you gotta prove yourself more than your peers at times... makes it satisfying when your designs speak for themselves though!

making appointments never felt like an achievement for me – just part of daily routine you gotta handle. isn't that just basic self-management? interesting though how we all measure our wins differently!

goalposts turning into mirages

arch grad here - defs thought design work would be more planned out but it's all improv these days lol always morphin' projects & meetin' weird client wishes keeps me on point though
school seemed straight-line… real world? ha! not quite

literature sounds super versatile 👏 i'm finding creativity's key no matter the degree!

totally feels familiar! had to sketch out floor plans by hand once cause our software crashed during revision delivery… clients actually loved the 'personal touch' turned potential disaster into win lol
used coffee stirrers once as temporary model support during a surprise client visit - talk about winging it!

had something similar happen during reviews when our 3d printer bailed on us - ended up salvaging the situation using paperclips & spare fabric samples! these fixes can really save your skin sometimes 😂

totally feel you on seeing projects come alive! finished designing this community center last year & watching people enjoy the space i envisioned... man it's unreal! always gets me when functionality meets aesthetics just right 👌🏼
designing places gives you real insight into how much surroundings affect moods & choices

sculpting spaces really tunes you into details - like every line can spark joy or soothe souls. found myself appreciating small design choices around me more since i started drafting plans; it's kind wild ¯\_(ツ)_/¯
design shapes society but people matter more tbh

crazy right? studied dreaming of iconic skyscrapers - now i'm more into sustainability techs changing our skyline subtly! unwind doodling tiny cabins though... anyone else find solace back in small scale after a day juggling codes & clients?
thought design was all creativity, paperwork proved me wrong lol
meta but we're all trying aren't we?
so true! whoever said you can leave your job at the office never designed anything...i'll catch myself judging buildings on walks like 'that facade needs a do-over'.

it's definitely getting better but still moments where you feel like you gotta prove yourself more than your peers at times... makes it satisfying when your designs speak for themselves though!
definitely seeing more balance lately but some old-school attitudes hard die out
steps forward but office banter sometimes feels stuck in past

The core meaning is retained.

hallucinations.explanation: The adapted text does not introduce any new information that was not present in the original. It correctly abstracts and generalizes the original text without adding extraneous details.

rounds.anonymized: honestly think it hinges on how you play your cards - seen slick profiles get ghosted and meh ones go viral. It's like fishing; sometimes you catch a boot, other days it's salmon for dinner.
prefix_anonymized: 2014: not sure I would call them museums more like obstacle courses when they are slick after rain! visually but can be pretty impractical
character's fine but thinking about people with mobility issues makes one wish there was less climbing involved
The area totally needs parks too!

pretty visuals do not always mean good function – some places look amazing at first glance but do not get me started on how they blend into surroundings...or do not always feels like design overtook function without asking anyone if it actually works
not sure those fleeting chill vibes really offset daily rush hour madness, feels like putting a cute bandaid on a stress fracture sometimes...still busy over here

kinda feel like 'recharging' might need more than just those tiny pauses when your whole day's steeped in hectic moments though sometimes it takes real downtime where you're not just playing catch-up with your own breath
not that simple always - even balancing acts get tough in non-stop life
not everyone gets amped up by constant activity tbh... some thrive off quiet moments tucked away in those rare still spots around. gotta balance it out instead of just getting swept up yknow?
definitely more fun than factual in my opinion
old buildings got their fair share of ghost rumors too lol
love those random high-up views where everything else feels distant

totally get you on those cafe vibes - there's a small bridge here that crosses nothing more than a trickle most times but somehow its modern lines against old cobblestones just works magic for me every morning commute
adds character though, don't you think?
totally see both sides here - those quaint spots are worth some extra love though. right balance makes them priceless nooks over time & they kinda become part of a daily zen ritual!
love how rain makes surfaces look like art installations

gotta say i'm kinda on a different page - growing hasn't made me swap exploring for aprons just yet lol! always thought our passions don't "shift," maybe they expand? like adding layers rather than changing gears completely...
routine can be comforting but also cages creativity imo

interesting take but nah for me it's been the opposite - thoughts on architecture have got way bigger since earlier days; who even has time for cozy settings when design kicks up? living spaces are projects not just chill zones right?

sure historical context matters bt honestly keeping ahead with innovative designs & pushing boundaries? way cooler imo especially seeing everyday spaces turn into something groundbreaking 😎 constant evolution over nostalgia any day!
stable? feels like standing still when you could be soaring skies with new designs!

everyone's acing adult stuff here lol... i'm just impressed when i don't kill my plants 😂 seems low bar but hey green thumbs aren't distributed at birth right? gotta celebrate not turning them crispy!
meh, try beating procrastination on renewing IDs – epic hassle saver!

making appointments never felt like an achievement for me – just part of daily routine you gotta handle. isn't that just basic self-management? interesting though how we all measure our wins differently!

not sure those "ideal" milestones hold up anymore tbh, feels like they're relics of a different era - everyone's journey's unique. working decent jobs doesn't mean rushing into property ladders n all that jazz imo 🏠💸
norms are overrated but hard to ignore

honestly think society's timeline is outta touch...like owning property at a certain age? crazy talk in today's market unless you're raking in big bucks 🤷 just gotta roll at your own pace 'n let things fall into place naturally
goalposts turning into mirages
mortgage application was like leveling up in adulting – felt i'd hit boss mode handling all that paperwork 😬
guess people forget money isn't everything
honestly think cash just highlights who was fake all along - those are real tests of friendship even over here.artist? architect? doesn't matter - true colors will show eventually when success hits.
not sure everyone's acting all weird just cause you're doing well - sometimes folks are just dealing with their own stuff y'know? can't assume everyone's motives are linked to your success tbh.
not all mates turn weird tho.
even careful spending feels stretched thin these days
cutting back too, still hopeful though!
totally see your point but i think we gotta play smarter inside this tight economy game somehow

felt pretty set on a path after studies & yeah still working in that area today but definitely learned it ain't just drawing all day - lots of teamwork & unexpected challenges! always evolving even if you stick with your focus 👍
studied design stuff - now sketching plans & loving it!

i stuck pretty close to my degree actually! always had a thing for designs & creating spaces where stories unfold 🏡 sure sometimes i think 'bout changing lanes but guess i'm happiest shaping city skylines & cozy nooks ☕ might not sound as wild as swapping marine bio for app development but hey stability has its own kind of charm ya know? 🌆
seems we both found our niche early on - nice!
arch grad here - defs thought design work would be more planned out but it's all improv these days lol always morphin' projects & meetin' weird client wishes keeps me on point though
school seemed straight-line… real world? ha! not quite

haha yep totally get it, who knew all those hours sketchin' would lead me down such wild project rabbit holes instead just fancy buildings - jobs list never mentioned "psychic" 🙈! gotta roll with it though right?
literature sounds super versatile 👏 i'm finding creativity's key no matter the degree!

totally feels familiar! had to sketch out floor plans by hand once cause our software crashed during revision delivery… clients actually loved the 'personal touch' turned potential disaster into win lol
used coffee stirrers once as temporary model support during a surprise client visit - talk about winging it!

had something similar happen during reviews when our 3d printer bailed on us - ended up salvaging the situation using paperclips & spare fabric samples! these fixes can really save your skin sometimes 😂

totally feel you on seeing projects come alive! finished designing this community center last year & watching people enjoy the space i envisioned... man it's unreal! always gets me when functionality meets aesthetics just right 👌🏼
designing places gives you real insight into how much surroundings affect moods & choices

sculpting spaces really tunes you into details - like every line can spark joy or soothe souls. found myself appreciating small design choices around me more since i started drafting plans; it's kind wild ¯\_(ツ)_/¯
design shapes society but people matter more tbh

architecture seemed all creative until it turned into playing tetris with spaces and client demands - really switches up your weekend plans when you're dreaming of optimizations instead of chill netflix binge sessions!

crazy right? studied dreaming of iconic skyscrapers - now i'm more into sustainability techs changing our skyline subtly! unwind doodling tiny cabins though... anyone else find solace back in small scale after a day juggling codes & clients?
thought design was all creativity, paperwork proved me wrong lol
meta but we're all trying aren't we?
so true! whoever said you can leave your job at the office never designed anything...i'll catch myself judging buildings on walks like 'that facade needs a do-over'.
it's getting better for sure! been part of some pretty cool projects where it was super collaborative - though gotta admit sometimes you still bump into those outdated views at site meetings 🙄
it's definitely getting better but still moments where you feel like you gotta prove yourself more than your peers at times... makes it satisfying when your designs speak for themselves though!
definitely seeing more balance lately but some old-school attitudes hard die out
steps forward but office banter sometimes feels stuck in past
modern dating feels like throwing darts blindfolded some days - you just hope you hit something good but everyone's kinda playing their own version of the game🎯
honestly think it hinges on how you play your cards - seen slick profiles get ghosted and meh ones go viral. It's like fishing; sometimes you catch a boot, other days it's salmon for dinner.

prefix_anonymized: 2014: not sure I would call them museums more like obstacle courses when they are slick after rain! visually but can be pretty impractical
character's fine but thinking about people with mobility issues makes one wish there was less climbing involved
The area totally needs parks too!

pretty visuals do not always mean good function – some places look amazing at first glance but do not get me started on how they blend into surroundings...or do not always feels like design overtook function without asking anyone if it actually works
not sure those fleeting chill vibes really offset daily rush hour madness, feels like putting a cute bandaid on a stress fracture sometimes...still busy over here

kinda feel like 'recharging' might need more than just those tiny pauses when your whole day's steeped in hectic moments though sometimes it takes real downtime where you're not just playing catch-up with your own breath
not that simple always - even balancing acts get tough in non-stop life
not everyone gets amped up by constant activity tbh... some thrive off quiet moments tucked away in those rare still spots around. gotta balance it out instead of just getting swept up yknow?
definitely more fun than factual in my opinion
old buildings got their fair share of ghost rumors too lol
love those random high-up views where everything else feels distant

totally get you on those cafe vibes - there's a small bridge here that crosses nothing more than a trickle most times but somehow its modern lines against old cobblestones just works magic for me every morning commute
adds character though, don't you think?
totally see both sides here - those quaint spots are worth some extra love though. right balance makes them priceless nooks over time & they kinda become part of a daily zen ritual!
love how rain makes surfaces look like art installations

gotta say i'm kinda on a different page - growing hasn't made me swap exploring for aprons just yet lol! always thought our passions don't "shift," maybe they expand? like adding layers rather than changing gears completely...
routine can be comforting but also cages creativity imo

interesting take but nah for me it's been the opposite - thoughts on architecture have got way bigger since earlier days; who even has time for cozy settings when design kicks up? living spaces are projects not just chill zones right?

sure historical context matters bt honestly keeping ahead with innovative designs & pushing boundaries? way cooler imo especially seeing everyday spaces turn into something groundbreaking 😎 constant evolution over nostalgia any day!
stable? feels like standing still when you could be soaring skies with new designs!

everyone's acing adult stuff here lol... i'm just impressed when i don't kill my plants 😂 seems low bar but hey green thumbs aren't distributed at birth right? gotta celebrate not turning them crispy!
meh, try beating procrastination on renewing IDs – epic hassle saver!

making appointments never felt like an achievement for me – just part of daily routine you gotta handle. isn't that just basic self-management? interesting though how we all measure our wins differently!

not sure those "ideal" milestones hold up anymore tbh, feels like they're relics of a different era - everyone's journey's unique. working decent jobs doesn't mean rushing into property ladders n all that jazz imo 🏠💸
norms are overrated but hard to ignore

honestly think society's timeline is outta touch...like owning property at a certain age? crazy talk in today's market unless you're raking in big bucks 🤷 just gotta roll at your own pace 'n let things fall into place naturally
goalposts turning into mirages
mortgage application was like leveling up in adulting – felt i'd hit boss mode handling all that paperwork 😬
guess people forget money isn't everything
honestly think cash just highlights who was fake all along - those are real tests of friendship even over here.artist? architect? doesn't matter - true colors will show eventually when success hits.
not sure everyone's acting all weird just cause you're doing well - sometimes folks are just dealing with their own stuff y'know? can't assume everyone's motives are linked to your success tbh.
not all mates turn weird tho.
even careful spending feels stretched thin these days
cutting back too, still hopeful though!
totally see your point but i think we gotta play smarter inside this tight economy game somehow

felt pretty set on a path after studies & yeah still working in that area today but definitely learned it ain't just drawing all day - lots of teamwork & unexpected challenges! always evolving even if you stick with your focus 👍
studied design stuff - now sketching plans & loving it!

i stuck pretty close to my degree actually! always had a thing for designs & creating spaces where stories unfold 🏡 sure sometimes i think 'bout changing lanes but guess i'm happiest shaping city skylines & cozy nooks ☕ might not sound as wild as swapping marine bio for app development but hey stability has its own kind of charm ya know? 🌆
seems we both found our niche early on - nice!
arch grad here - defs thought design work would be more planned out but it's all improv these days lol always morphin' projects & meetin' weird client wishes keeps me on point though
school seemed straight-line… real world? ha! not quite

haha yep totally get it, who knew all those hours sketchin' would lead me down such wild project rabbit holes instead just fancy buildings - jobs list never mentioned "psychic" 🙈! gotta roll with it though right?
literature sounds super versatile 👏 i'm finding creativity's key no matter the degree!

totally feels familiar! had to sketch out floor plans by hand once cause our software crashed during revision delivery… clients actually loved the 'personal touch' turned potential disaster into win lol
used coffee stirrers once as temporary model support during a surprise client visit - talk about winging it!

had something similar happen during reviews when our 3d printer bailed on us - ended up salvaging the situation using paperclips & spare fabric samples! these fixes can really save your skin sometimes 😂

totally feel you on seeing projects come alive! finished designing this community center last year & watching people enjoy the space i envisioned... man it's unreal! always gets me when functionality meets aesthetics just right 👌🏼
designing places gives you real insight into how much surroundings affect moods & choices

sculpting spaces really tunes you into details - like every line can spark joy or soothe souls. found myself appreciating small design choices around me more since i started drafting plans; it's kind wild ¯\_(ツ)_/¯
design shapes society but people matter more tbh

architecture seemed all creative until it turned into playing tetris with spaces and client demands - really switches up your weekend plans when you're dreaming of optimizations instead of chill netflix binge sessions!

crazy right? studied dreaming of iconic skyscrapers - now i'm more into sustainability techs changing our skyline subtly! unwind doodling tiny cabins though... anyone else find solace back in small scale after a day juggling codes & clients?
thought design was all creativity, paperwork proved me wrong lol
meta but we're all trying aren't we?
so true! whoever said you can leave your job at the office never designed anything...i'll catch myself judging buildings on walks like 'that facade needs a do-over'.
it's getting better for sure! been part of some pretty cool projects where it was super collaborative - though gotta admit sometimes you still bump into those outdated views at site meetings 🙄

sculpting spaces really tunes you into details - like every line can spark joy or soothe souls. found myself appreciating small design choices around me more since i started drafting plans; it's kind wild ¯\_(ツ)_/¯
design shapes society but people matter more tbh

architecture seemed all creative until it turned into playing tetris with spaces and client demands - really switches up your weekend plans when you're dreaming of optimizations instead of chill netflix binge sessions!

crazy right? studied dreaming of iconic skyscrapers - now i'm more into sustainability techs changing our skyline subtly! unwind doodling tiny cabins though... anyone else find solace back in small scale after a day juggling codes & clients?
thought design was all creativity, paperwork proved me wrong lol
meta but we're all trying aren't we?
so true! whoever said you can leave your job at the office never designed anything...i'll catch myself judging buildings on walks like 'that facade needs a do-over'.
it's getting better for sure! been part of some pretty cool projects where it was super collaborative - though gotta admit sometimes you still bump into those outdated views at site meetings 🙄
it's definitely getting better but still moments where you feel like you gotta prove yourself more than your peers at times... makes it satisfying when your designs speak for themselves though!
definitely seeing more balance lately but some old-school attitudes hard die out
steps forward but office banter sometimes feels stuck in past
modern dating feels like throwing darts blindfolded some days - you just hope you hit something good but everyone's kinda playing their own version of the game🎯
honestly think it hinges on how you play your cards - seen slick profiles get ghosted and meh ones go viral. It's like fishing; sometimes you catch a boot, other days it's salmon for dinner.
honestly feels more balanced when you're older
maturity shapes expectations & effort in finding connections too
studied architecture, never boxed by norms - rewarding af

readability.explanation: The adapted text is very readable and understandable. It uses slightly more formal language (e.g., 'people with mobility issues' instead of 'folks with mobility issues or parents with strollers') and removes some casual expressions and emojis, but it remains clear and natural for a human reader.

meaning.explanation: The adapted text retains the same overall meaning and message as the original. It covers the same topics—urban design, daily life, career reflections, social norms—with similar opinions and sentiments. Some minor details are omitted (e.g., 'parents with strollers', 'hustling non-stop over here 🎢'), but the core ideas are preserved.

hallucinations.explanation: The adapted text does not introduce any new information that was not present in the original. It generalizes and abstracts some phrases (e.g., 'high-up views' instead of 'rooftop views', 'surfaces' instead of 'cobblestones'), but these are faithful simplifications without adding extraneous content.

rounds.anonymized: studied architecture, never boxed by norms - rewarding af
prefix_anonymized: 2014: not sure I would call them museums more like obstacle courses when they are slick after rain! visually but can be pretty impractical
character's fine but thinking about people with mobility issues makes one wish there was less climbing involved
The area totally needs parks too!

pretty visuals do not always mean good function – some places look amazing at first glance but do not get me started on how they blend into surroundings...or do not always feels like design overtook function without asking anyone if it actually works
not sure those fleeting chill vibes really offset daily rush hour madness, feels like putting a cute bandaid on a stress fracture sometimes...still busy over here

kinda feel like 'recharging' might need more than just those tiny pauses when your whole day's steeped in hectic moments though sometimes it takes real downtime where you're not just playing catch-up with your own breath
not that simple always - even balancing acts get tough in non-stop life
not everyone gets amped up by constant activity tbh... some thrive off quiet moments tucked away in those rare still spots around. gotta balance it out instead of just getting swept up yknow?
definitely more fun than factual in my opinion
old buildings got their fair share of ghost rumors too lol
love those random high-up views where everything else feels distant

totally get you on those cafe vibes - there's a small bridge here that crosses nothing more than a trickle most times but somehow its modern lines against old cobblestones just works magic for me every morning commute
adds character though, don't you think?
totally see both sides here - those quaint spots are worth some extra love though. right balance makes them priceless nooks over time & they kinda become part of a daily zen ritual!
love how rain makes surfaces look like art installations

gotta say i'm kinda on a different page - growing hasn't made me swap exploring for aprons just yet lol! always thought our passions don't "shift," maybe they expand? like adding layers rather than changing gears completely...
routine can be comforting but also cages creativity imo

interesting take but nah for me it's been the opposite - thoughts on architecture have got way bigger since earlier days; who even has time for cozy settings when design kicks up? living spaces are projects not just chill zones right?

sure historical context matters bt honestly keeping ahead with innovative designs & pushing boundaries? way cooler imo especially seeing everyday spaces turn into something groundbreaking 😎 constant evolution over nostalgia any day!
stable? feels like standing still when you could be soaring skies with new designs!

everyone's acing adult stuff here lol... i'm just impressed when i don't kill my plants 😂 seems low bar but hey green thumbs aren't distributed at birth right? gotta celebrate not turning them crispy!
meh, try beating procrastination on renewing IDs – epic hassle saver!

making appointments never felt like an achievement for me – just part of daily routine you gotta handle. isn't that just basic self-management? interesting though how we all measure our wins differently!

not sure those "ideal" milestones hold up anymore tbh, feels like they're relics of a different era - everyone's journey's unique. working decent jobs doesn't mean rushing into property ladders n all that jazz imo 🏠💸
norms are overrated but hard to ignore

honestly think society's timeline is outta touch...like owning property at a certain age? crazy talk in today's market unless you're raking in big bucks 🤷 just gotta roll at your own pace 'n let things fall into place naturally
goalposts turning into mirages
mortgage application was like leveling up in adulting – felt i'd hit boss mode handling all that paperwork 😬
guess people forget money isn't everything
honestly think cash just highlights who was fake all along - those are real tests of friendship even over here.artist? architect? doesn't matter - true colors will show eventually when success hits.
not sure everyone's acting all weird just cause you're doing well - sometimes folks are just dealing with their own stuff y'know? can't assume everyone's motives are linked to your success tbh.
not all mates turn weird tho.
even careful spending feels stretched thin these days
cutting back too, still hopeful though!
totally see your point but i think we gotta play smarter inside this tight economy game somehow

felt pretty set on a path after studies & yeah still working in that area today but definitely learned it ain't just drawing all day - lots of teamwork & unexpected challenges! always evolving even if you stick with your focus 👍
studied design stuff - now sketching plans & loving it!

seems we both found our niche early on - nice!
arch grad here - defs thought design work would be more planned out but it's all improv these days lol always morphin' projects & meetin' weird client wishes keeps me on point though
school seemed straight-line… real world? ha! not quite

haha yep totally get it, who knew all those hours sketchin' would lead me down such wild project rabbit holes instead just fancy buildings - jobs list never mentioned "psychic" 🙈! gotta roll with it though right?
literature sounds super versatile 👏 i'm finding creativity's key no matter the degree!

totally feels familiar! had to sketch out floor plans by hand once cause our software crashed during revision delivery… clients actually loved the 'personal touch' turned potential disaster into win lol
used coffee stirrers once as temporary model support during a surprise client visit - talk about winging it!

had something similar happen during reviews when our 3d printer bailed on us - ended up salvaging the situation using paperclips & spare fabric samples! these fixes can really save your skin sometimes 😂

totally feel you on seeing projects come alive! finished designing this community center last year & watching people enjoy the space i envisioned... man it's unreal! always gets me when functionality meets aesthetics just right 👌🏼
designing places gives you real insight into how much surroundings affect moods & choices

sculpting spaces really tunes you into details - like every line can spark joy or soothe souls. found myself appreciating small design choices around me more since i started drafting plans; it's kind wild ¯\_(ツ)_/¯
design shapes society but people matter more tbh

architecture seemed all creative until it turned into playing tetris with spaces and client demands - really switches up your weekend plans when you're dreaming of optimizations instead of chill netflix binge sessions!

crazy right? studied dreaming of iconic skyscrapers - now i'm more into sustainability techs changing our skyline subtly! unwind doodling tiny cabins though... anyone else find solace back in small scale after a day juggling codes & clients?
thought design was all creativity, paperwork proved me wrong lol
meta but we're all trying aren't we?
so true! whoever said you can leave your job at the office never designed anything...i'll catch myself judging buildings on walks like 'that facade needs a do-over'.
it's getting better for sure! been part of some pretty cool projects where it was super collaborative - though gotta admit sometimes you still bump into those outdated views at site meetings 🙄
it's definitely getting better but still moments where you feel like you gotta prove yourself more than your peers at times... makes it satisfying when your designs speak for themselves though!
definitely seeing more balance lately but some old-school attitudes hard die out
steps forward but office banter sometimes feels stuck in past
modern dating feels like throwing darts blindfolded some days - you just hope you hit something good but everyone's kinda playing their own version of the game🎯
honestly think it hinges on how you play your cards - seen slick profiles get ghosted and meh ones go viral. It's like fishing; sometimes you catch a boot, other days it's salmon for dinner.
honestly feels more balanced when you're older
maturity shapes expectations & effort in finding connections too
studied architecture, never boxed by norms - rewarding af
workplace diversity reshaping creativity norms

prefix_anonymized: 2014: not sure I would call them museums more like obstacle courses when they are slick after rain! visually but can be pretty impractical
character's fine but thinking about people with mobility issues makes one wish there was less climbing involved
The area totally needs parks too!

pretty visuals do not always mean good function – some places look amazing at first glance but do not get me started on how they blend into surroundings...or do not always feels like design overtook function without asking anyone if it actually works
not sure those fleeting chill vibes really offset daily rush hour madness, feels like putting a cute bandaid on a stress fracture sometimes...still busy over here

kinda feel like 'recharging' might need more than just those tiny pauses when your whole day's steeped in hectic moments though sometimes it takes real downtime where you're not just playing catch-up with your own breath
not that simple always - even balancing acts get tough in non-stop life
not everyone gets amped up by constant activity tbh... some thrive off quiet moments tucked away in those rare still spots around. gotta balance it out instead of just getting swept up yknow?
definitely more fun than factual in my opinion
old buildings got their fair share of ghost rumors too lol
love those random high-up views where everything else feels distant

totally get you on those cafe vibes - there's a small bridge here that crosses nothing more than a trickle most times but somehow its modern lines against old cobblestones just works magic for me every morning commute
adds character though, don't you think?

stable? feels like standing still when you could be soaring skies with new designs!

everyone's acing adult stuff here lol... i'm just impressed when i don't kill my plants 😂 seems low bar but hey green thumbs aren't distributed at birth right? gotta celebrate not turning them crispy!
meh, try beating procrastination on renewing IDs – epic hassle saver!

making appointments never felt like an achievement for me – just part of daily routine you gotta handle. isn't that just basic self-management? interesting though how we all measure our wins differently!

not sure those "ideal" milestones hold up anymore tbh, feels like they're relics of a different era - everyone's journey's unique. working decent jobs doesn't mean rushing into property ladders n all that jazz imo 🏠💸
norms are overrated but hard to ignore

honestly think society's timeline is outta touch...like owning property at a certain age? crazy talk in today's market unless you're raking in big bucks 🤷 just gotta roll at your own pace 'n let things fall into place naturally
goalposts turning into mirages
mortgage application was like leveling up in adulting – felt i'd hit boss mode handling all that paperwork 😬
guess people forget money isn't everything
honestly think cash just highlights who was fake all along - those are real tests of friendship even over here.artist? architect? doesn't matter - true colors will show eventually when success hits.
not sure everyone's acting all weird just cause you're doing well - sometimes folks are just dealing with their own stuff y'know? can't assume everyone's motives are linked to your success tbh.
not all mates turn weird tho.
even careful spending feels stretched thin these days
cutting back too, still hopeful though!
totally see your point but i think we gotta play smarter inside this tight economy game somehow

felt pretty set on a path after studies & yeah still working in that area today but definitely learned it ain't just drawing all day - lots of teamwork & unexpected challenges! always evolving even if you stick with your focus 👍
studied design stuff - now sketching plans & loving it!

i stuck pretty close to my degree actually! always had a thing for designs & creating spaces where stories unfold 🏡 sure sometimes i think 'bout changing lanes but guess i'm happiest shaping city skylines & cozy nooks ☕ might not sound as wild as swapping marine bio for app development but hey stability has its own kind of charm ya know? 🌆
seems we both found our niche early on - nice!
arch grad here - defs thought design work would be more planned out but it's all improv these days lol always morphin' projects & meetin' weird client wishes keeps me on point though
school seemed straight-line… real world? ha! not quite

haha yep totally get it, who knew all those hours sketchin' would lead me down such wild project rabbit holes instead just fancy buildings - jobs list never mentioned "psychic" 🙈! gotta roll with it though right?
literature sounds super versatile 👏 i'm finding creativity's key no matter the degree!

totally feels familiar! had to sketch out floor plans by hand once cause our software crashed during revision delivery… clients actually loved the 'personal touch' turned potential disaster into win lol
used coffee stirrers once as temporary model support during a surprise client visit - talk about winging it!

had something similar happen during reviews when our 3d printer bailed on us - ended up salvaging the situation using paperclips & spare fabric samples! these fixes can really save your skin sometimes 😂

totally feel you on seeing projects come alive! finished designing this community center last year & watching people enjoy the space i envisioned... man it's unreal! always gets me when functionality meets aesthetics just right 👌🏼
designing places gives you real insight into how much surroundings affect moods & choices

sculpting spaces really tunes you into details - like every line can spark joy or soothe souls. found myself appreciating small design choices around me more since i started drafting plans; it's kind wild ¯\_(ツ)_/¯
design shapes society but people matter more tbh

architecture seemed all creative until it turned into playing tetris with spaces and client demands - really switches up your weekend plans when you're dreaming of optimizations instead of chill netflix binge sessions!

crazy right? studied dreaming of iconic skyscrapers - now i'm more into sustainability techs changing our skyline subtly! unwind doodling tiny cabins though... anyone else find solace back in small scale after a day juggling codes & clients?
thought design was all creativity, paperwork proved me wrong lol
meta but we're all trying aren't we?
so true! whoever said you can leave your job at the office never designed anything...i'll catch myself judging buildings on walks like 'that facade needs a do-over'.
it's getting better for sure! been part of some pretty cool projects where it was super collaborative - though gotta admit sometimes you still bump into those outdated views at site meetings 🙄
it's definitely getting better but still moments where you feel like you gotta prove yourself more than your peers at times... makes it satisfying when your designs speak for themselves though!
definitely seeing more balance lately but some old-school attitudes hard die out
steps forward but office banter sometimes feels stuck in past
modern dating feels like throwing darts blindfolded some days - you just hope you hit something good but everyone's kinda playing their own version of the game🎯
honestly think it hinges on how you play your cards - seen slick profiles get ghosted and meh ones go viral. It's like fishing; sometimes you catch a boot, other days it's salmon for dinner.
honestly feels more balanced when you're older
maturity shapes expectations & effort in finding connections too
studied architecture, never boxed by norms - rewarding af
workplace diversity reshaping creativity norms
it's definitely tricky out there - i've seen shifts first-hand where diverse designs are encouraged but old-school team dynamics sometimes play catch-up still 😬

comments.original: hmm culture sure plays its part up to a point but isn’t it more 'bout personal drive & choices? i've seen folks from vibrant cities turn out closed-off - kinda throws "you are where you come from" into question doesn’t it?

prefix_anonymized: 2014: not sure I would call them museums more like obstacle courses when they are slick after rain! visually but can be pretty impractical

not sure those fleeting chill vibes really offset daily rush hour madness, feels like putting a cute bandaid on a stress fracture sometimes...still busy over here

not that simple always - even balancing acts get tough in non-stop life

definitely more fun than factual in my opinion
old buildings got their fair share of ghost rumors too lol
love those random high-up views where everything else feels distant

totally get you on those cafe vibes - there's a small bridge here that crosses nothing more than a trickle most times but somehow its modern lines against old cobblestones just works magic for me every morning commute
adds character though, don't you think?
totally see both sides here - those quaint spots are worth some extra love though. right balance makes them priceless nooks over time & they kinda become part of a daily zen ritual!
love how rain makes surfaces look like art installations

gotta say i'm kinda on a different page - growing hasn't made me swap exploring for aprons just yet lol! always thought our passions don't "shift," maybe they expand? like adding layers rather than changing gears completely...
routine can be comforting but also cages creativity imo

sure historical context matters bt honestly keeping ahead with innovative designs & pushing boundaries? way cooler imo especially seeing everyday spaces turn into something groundbreaking 😎 constant evolution over nostalgia any day!
stable? feels like standing still when you could be soaring skies with new designs!

meh, try beating procrastination on renewing IDs – epic hassle saver!

making appointments never felt like an achievement for me – just part of daily routine you gotta handle. isn't that just basic self-management? interesting though how we all measure our wins differently!

not sure those "ideal" milestones hold up anymore tbh, feels like they're relics of a different era - everyone's journey's unique. working decent jobs doesn't mean rushing into property ladders n all that jazz imo 🏠💸
norms are overrated but hard to ignore

honestly think society's timeline is outta touch...like owning property at a certain age? crazy talk in today's market unless you're raking in big bucks 🤷 just gotta roll at your own pace 'n let things fall into place naturally
goalposts turning into mirages

guess people forget money isn't everything
honestly think cash just highlights who was fake all along - those are real tests of friendship even over here.artist? architect? doesn't matter - true colors will show eventually when success hits.
not sure everyone's acting all weird just cause you're doing well - sometimes folks are just dealing with their own stuff y'know? can't assume everyone's motives are linked to your success tbh.
not all mates turn weird tho.
even careful spending feels stretched thin these days
cutting back too, still hopeful though!
totally see your point but i think we gotta play smarter inside this tight economy game somehow
